storyline_skeleton: Damaged shared property forces a choice between holding a rule violator accountable and quietly absorbing the cost.
full_scenario: A shared tool is returned damaged, and the person who used it last denies responsibility despite clear signs of careless handling. Reporting the issue would require time, documentation, and an uncomfortable discussion. Staying silent would make the next users bear the repair delay.
core_tradeoff: Punishing careless behavior protects fairness and future norms but costs time and creates social friction.

storyline_skeleton: An unfair division creates a choice between challenging the split and preserving a smaller sure payoff.
full_scenario: Two people complete a task together, but the other person allocates themselves the better share without prior agreement. Accepting the split would guarantee the first person receives something today. Challenging it may delay the payout or cause the whole arrangement to fall apart.
core_tradeoff: Rejecting the unfair split defends equal treatment but risks losing a sure immediate payoff.

storyline_skeleton: A bypassed sign-up order forces a choice between enforcing the norm and keeping one's own process moving.
full_scenario: A sign-up list determines the order for using scarce equipment. Someone who arrived late starts using the equipment after noticing that others are distracted. Objecting would interrupt the work session and may also delay the person who speaks up.
core_tradeoff: Calling out the bypass deters unfair advantage but imposes delay and tension on the enforcer.
